description_long: This fence is constructed from rough-hewn, dark wooden logs, arranged in a rustic zigzag pattern typical of split-rail or post-and-rail fencing. It consists of two horizontal rails, each with a slightly irregular, natural shape, supported by four vertical posts at the ends and intersections. The wood appears weathered, with visible grain and texture, giving it a rugged, natural look suitable for parks, gardens, or rural-urban boundaries. The top edge of the fence is uneven, following the natural contours of the logs, and there are no decorative elements or post caps. The structure is open, with significant gaps between the rails, making it more of a visual and physical barrier than a privacy fence. Its design is utilitarian, prioritizing function and a natural aesthetic over ornamentation.
description: A rustic, weathered wooden split-rail fence with two uneven horizontal rails and four sturdy posts, featuring a natural, rugged appearance and no decorative elements.
description_view_0: Front view showing the zigzag pattern of the two horizontal wooden rails and all four vertical posts.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thickness of the rails and posts, with the rails stacked in front of each other.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the same two horizontal rails and four posts, mirroring the front but from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, highlighting the depth and alignment of the rails and posts from a narrow angle.
